food_subgroup: Toasted bread appetisers with seafood topping

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bruschetta food product class; no specific crab bruschetta subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW covers bruschetta as a category; no crab-specific bruschetta code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for bruschetta or crab bruschetta as a food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is 6 pieces of bruschetta, each approximately 30 g, totalling approximately 180 g, as served at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore.

nutrient_source.title: Crab salad (crabmeat, mayonnaise) and toasted bread composite — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Bread, Italian (NDB 18033), Crab, blue, cooked (NDB 15139), Mayonnaise, regular (NDB 04025), Mustard, prepared (NDB 02046), Cucumber, raw (NDB 11206), Tomatoes, red, raw (NDB 11529)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for bruschetta al granchio exists in any approved primary source. Values estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for toasted Italian bread (~55% by weight), crabmeat (~20%), mayonnaise (~15%), mustard (~2%), cucumber (~4%), and tomato (~4%). Proportions derived from standard recipe construction for this dish type. Energy cross-checked against Atwater factors.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread and mixed seafood-topped bread analogues; Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bruschetta al granchio. GI estimated at 53 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle being toasted Italian white bread (GI ~55 per University of Sydney GI Database for white bread, toasted) with a moderate protein and fat load from crabmeat and mayonnaise, which is well-established to lower the effective GI of the composite meal. Composite GI estimated conservatively at 53 (low category, ≤55). Glycemic load per 180 g serving = (53 × 38.7 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 20.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~390 mg/100 g; ~702 mg per 180 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as a single 6-piece serving provides approximately 702 mg sodium (~30% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), driven by mayonnaise, mustard, and crabmeat; portion reduction or a low-sodium mayonnaise substitution is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from toasted white bread base (GI ~53, GL ~20.5 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the toasted white bread base contributes a moderate glycemic load (~20.5 per serving); pairing with the protein and fat from crabmeat and mayonnaise attenuates the glycemic response, but portion control remains important to manage postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content with saturated fat from mayonnaise and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the combination of ~9.1 g fat/100 g (predominantly from mayonnaise), ~390 mg sodium/100 g, and ~38 mg cholesterol/100 g warrants moderation; choosing a reduced-fat mayonnaise and limiting serving size to 3 pieces can meaningfully reduce saturated fat and sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bruschetta al Granchio is an Italian appetiser consisting of six slices of toasted bread — typically a baguette or ciabatta — topped with a cold crabmeat mixture. The topping is made from crabmeat bound with mayonnaise and mustard, garnished with finely diced cucumber and tomato cubes, giving it a creamy, slightly tangy flavour with a fresh, crunchy contrast from the vegetables. The toasted bread provides a crisp base that contrasts with the soft, rich topping. In Singapore, this dish is served at Italian restaurants such as Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore and is positioned as a starter or sharing plate. Nutritionally, each 180 g serving (6 pieces) provides approximately 356 kcal, 14.8 g protein, 16.4 g fat, and 38.7 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at approximately 702 mg per serving, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low category), moderated by the protein and fat content of the topping. Crabmeat contributes vitamin B12, zinc, and selenium. The dish contains common allergens including gluten, crustaceans, eggs (from mayonnaise), and mustard.
common_user_queries: How many calories in crab bruschetta?
Is bruschetta al granchio high in sodium?
What allergens are in bruschetta al granchio?
How much protein in crab bruschetta?
What is the glycemic index of bruschetta?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for bruschetta al granchio exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values were estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for the component ingredients using standard recipe proportions. GI was estimated by analogy to toasted white bread with protein-fat moderation. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA SR Legacy data is high quality and the recipe construction is well-constrained by the dish description.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite for this dish type and is not reliably present in the component ingredients at measurable levels in the source database. Serving size of 180 g (6 × 30 g pieces) is estimated from standard bruschetta portion conventions; the restaurant menu does not publish a gram weight. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 53 × available carbohydrate 38.7 g per 180 g serving) / 100 = 20.5. Halal status is not confirmed as the restaurant has not published halal certification; is_halal set to false by default. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite and should be treated as indicative rather than analytically confirmed values.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with USDA SR Legacy composite nutrient estimation. Identified vitamin_d_mcg as -1 requiring documentation in notes.

Iteration 2: Added explicit -1 sentinel documentation for vitamin_d_mcg in notes per G3 rule. Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. Confirmed GI 53 ≤ 55 correctly classified as low. Confirmed all match_type values use only permitted strings. Confirmed zero-macro justification not needed as all core macros are non-zero. Confirmed G6 compliance: Italian cuisine does not require Singaporean multilingual coverage; Chinese names provided as courtesy for Singapore context.
